topic: Information density versus generous whitespace (expert versus novice users)
competing_claims: Generous whitespace improves readability, reduces errors and feels modern, so interfaces should be airy.
Expert and operational users are slowed by low-density layouts and prefer compact, information-rich screens that minimise scrolling and navigation.
resolution: Match density to expertise and frequency of use. Novice, infrequent and consumer audiences benefit from lower density and more whitespace; domain professionals doing high-frequency operational work benefit from higher density and at-a-glance data. Offer density controls where one product serves both audiences, and never sacrifice legibility, target size or grouping to achieve density.
unresolved: Where exactly the density threshold sits for 'mixed' audiences is not settled and needs per-product testing.
